sources.label: Romande Energie press releases 2026 (last item May 27 AGM; nothing June 5-9)
Investing.com - Romande Energie 2025 results / Vision 2040 (Mar 31, 2026, for thesis context)

verdicts.rankRationale: No company news since June 6 (last release May 27 AGM; dividend paid June 2), so the scaling-page thesis of utility exposure to electricity demand growth is unchanged at rank 11. Today's +2.1% and YTD creep from +10.4% to +12.7% is newsless drift that very slightly erodes residual alpha, not a setup change.
verdicts.bull: Vision 2040 (5 TWh renewables, 60% EBITDA jump in 2025) plus Swiss grid/electrification demand gives a $1.6B under-owned regulated compounder real torque if Romandie data-center and electrification load materializes.
verdicts.neutral: A small regulated Swiss utility grinding up a couple percent on no news; the electricity-demand thesis is intact but unproven by any fresh contract or load-growth datapoint since the March results.
verdicts.bear: YTD +12.7% without a single new fundamental datapoint since March means the easy rerating is partly spent; regulated tariffs and hydro variability cap upside if Swiss power demand growth disappoints.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: H1 2026 results / any disclosed large-load or data-center supply agreement in French-speaking Switzerland evidencing demand-growth inflection.
verdicts.primaryRisk: Newsless multiple creep reverses; regulated-return caps and weak Swiss power prices make the electricity-demand-growth thesis slow to show up in earnings.
